exemplar.title: How does Carol Ann Duffy transform traditional fairy tales to empower women in the poem anthology The Worlds Wife?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Carol Ann Duffy transform traditional fairy tales to empower women in the poem anthology The Worlds Wife?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Demonstrates persuasive and nuanced interpretation of feminist themes in both Little Red Cap and Mrs Beast
Consistent, well-embedded textual references and quotes that effectively support arguments
Shows detailed understanding of narrative revisions, imagery and context within third-wave feminism
weaknesses: Lacks full bibliographic precision (publication details, line numbers) in some citations
Could broaden discussion with wider collection perspective and deeper historical context
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although a Band 4 was originally awarded, the essayâs sustained and generally persuasive interpretation of feminist implications, together with well-chosen and grammatically embedded quotations, merits the top mark. Minor omissions in publication details and contextual breadth keep it from flawless, but overall the knowledge and interpretation are excellent.

strengths: Insightful analysis of intertextual links (e.g., The Little Mermaid) and metaphorical readings (forest as journey)
Appropriate and at times convincing evaluation of poetic devices like anaphora and caesura to show female agency
Strong, original readings (ace of spades as vulvic symbol; poker as feminist space)
weaknesses: Some observations remain descriptive without deeper unpacking
Technical terms and ironic effects occasionally asserted rather than fully explored
Evaluation is uneven, with surface-level passages alongside stronger insights
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay moves beyond mere description to offer appropriate and at times insightful evaluations of authorial choices. While not consistently at the highest level of depth, its strengths in metaphorical and intertextual analysis justify elevating to Band 4.

strengths: Clear, logical structure with distinct sections for each poem and a coherent line of inquiry
Mostly effective integration of supporting examples into the argument
Consistent topic sentences and progression maintain reader engagement
weaknesses: Occasional tangents dilute focus and some sentences or paragraphs are incomplete
The conclusion is truncated and does not fully restate or resolve the thesis
A few standalone quotations lack analysis and disrupt flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Organization is well handled, with a cohesive line of inquiry and mostly smooth integration of evidence. Minor structural lapses and an underdeveloped conclusion limit this to a strong Band 4 rather than the top level.

strengths: Language is consistently clear with varied and appropriate academic vocabulary
Grammar and sentence construction are accurate overall, supporting complex analysis
Register and style are consistently appropriate to a critical essay task
weaknesses: Minor typographical and punctuation errors persist (e.g., âiniusticeâ)
Occasional awkward phrasing and repetitive word choice
Works Cited formatting and URL presentation need refinement for full MLA compliance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essayâs language is clear, precise and largely error-free, sustaining an academic register throughout. Small lapses in minor errors and stylistic consistency prevent a Band 5, but the performance clearly sits at Band 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Quotations are mostly woven into sentences (âThe anaphoric rhyme of âyear in, year outâ¦ââ) and analysed within the same paragraph, indicating **mostly effective integration**. Occasional stand-alone lines or block quotes without commentary appear, preventing the work from reaching the seamless integration of Band 5, but overall performance aligns with Band 4 expectations.

The student demonstrates **solid familiarity with both Little Red Cap and Mrs Beast**âsummarising key narrative revisions, thematic concerns (empowerment, patriarchy) and contextualising the collection within third-wave feminism. Commentary shows an awareness of how setting, imagery and plot reversals differ from the Grimm originals and how these differences advance feminist aims. While this reveals good insight, the discussion stops short of the sustained, fully nuanced or wider-collection perspective that would justify an âexcellentâ rating. Hence âgood knowledge and understandingâ (Band 4).

The essay follows a clear structure: introduction with thesis, separate sections on each poem, comparative concluding overview. Topic sentences and logical progression reflect **sound organization and cohesion**. The line of inquiry (transformation of fairy tales to empower women) is maintained, though occasional tangents into contextual remarks dilute focus slightly; still, development is adequate for Band 4.

Quotations such as âWhat big ears he had!â and âscratch my back till it bledâ are **frequent, relevant and usually embedded grammatically**, supporting individual points effectively. Citations from outside sources are minimal but acceptable for an HL essay. A few references are paraphrased rather than quoted, and page/line numbers are absent, keeping the work just below the precision expected for Band 5. Therefore Band 4 is the best fit.

The essay sustains an **academic register** with analytical verbs and nominal style, rarely lapsing into informality despite quoting colloquial diction from the poems. Tone suits the critical task, citation style is conventional, and formality is maintained, matching the âconsistently appropriateâ descriptor of Band 4.

Language is **consistently clear and mostly error-free**; vocabulary (e.g., âpatriarchal hegemony,â âanachronisticâ) is appropriate and varied. Minor slips (âiniustice,â missing commas) and occasional awkward phrasing keep accuracy from being âvery highâ, but the overall clarity and command satisfy Band 4.

The student comments on rhyme (âinternal rhyme schemeâ), anaphora, metaphor and caesura and links these devices to female agency, showing **generally appropriate literary analysis**. However, several observations remain descriptive (e.g., labelling imagery as âthreateningâ without further exploration) and some technical terms are asserted rather than unpacked. The evaluation is unevenâinsightful in places (ace of spades as vulvic symbol) but surface-level elsewhereâplacing it in the âadequate/generally appropriateâ range, Band 3.

annotations.comment: The introduction clearly states the research question, but the thesis could be more specific by mentioning both poems by name earlier to guide the reader.
Defining the third wave of feminism in greater detail would strengthen context and deepen understanding of Duffyâs engagement with feminist discourse.
Several quotations lack page or line numbers. Including precise references would improve academic rigor and allow verification.
Insightful observation of the anaphoric repetition (âWhat big ears he had! â¦â) effectively links form to the protagonistâs awakening agency.
The phrase âdestroy her innocence and purityâ is awkward. Consider rephrasing for clarity, for example âshed her innocenceâ or ârenounce her purity.â
Strong metaphorical reading of the forest setting as a symbol of dangerous exploration that mirrors the protagonistâs journey.
To deepen analysis, explicitly link the threatening forest imagery to broader feminist themes of danger and liberation.
The paragraph ends abruptly with âglistening, virgin white ofâ. Has text been omitted? Check for missing content to maintain coherence.
Integrate the quotation âtook an axe to the wolf as he sleptâ into your own analysis rather than presenting it standalone.
Include line numbers for the rhyme âyear in, year out, season after seasonâ to bolster textual precision.
Typographical error in âiniustice.â Correct spelling to âinjusticeâ to maintain academic accuracy.
The intertextual link to The Little Mermaid insightfully broadens Duffyâs critique of patriarchal expectations.
Unpack the ironic effect of the interjections âgood-â¦ better-â¦ best-â in greater depth to reveal Duffyâs satirical stance.
Discuss the connotation of âbestâ regarding sexual objectification versus empowerment to deepen semantic analysis.
The sentence ending with âas if she'sâ is incomplete. Ensure each sentence is fully developed for clarity.
The portrayal of masochism in Mrs Beast effectively subverts expectations and highlights Duffyâs provocative style.
Strong reading of the poker game as a feminist space, celebrating sisterhood rather than male competition.
Consider adding historical context on pokerâs masculine connotations to reinforce how Duffy subverts tradition.
The final paragraph is truncated and does not fully conclude the argument. Ensure the conclusion reiterates the central thesis.
To strengthen coherence, restate how each poemâs examples collectively support the overarching feminist argument.
The prologue analysis effectively frames Duffyâs intent and reinforces the essayâs line of inquiry.
The word âpowerfulâ appears multiple times. Vary your vocabulary (e.g., formidable, influential) to avoid repetition.
Integrate brief commentary after each quoted example in the prologue to strengthen the link between evidence and evaluation.
Primary text citation is formatted correctly in MLA style, demonstrating attention to bibliographic conventions.
The Pruitt entry lacks a clear publication date and place. Include full publication details to meet MLA standards.
Ensure the URL in the Works Cited follows MLA formatting (e.g., preceded by âAccessedâ date or hyperlink format).
